content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch delta against `453f57bf5a70cd69f9a3fb0345fadc4bcf80f6fb` and found 3 fix-now correctness/robustness gaps in the nearby-work builder/bootstrap path.

## Amendments

1. `src/cli/project.rs:3290-3323` hard-filters `related_work` candidates to the active milestone, so a bead in another milestone that shares the same labels/subsystem never appears in `## Nearby work`. That breaks the main purpose of this bead for cross-milestone ownership: the final-review router already accepts canonical nearby IDs from any milestone (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:560-584`), but the prompt builder prevents those owners from ever being surfaced. Reviewers will misclassify those findings as `fix-now` or `propose-new-bead` because the owning bead is missing from the only prompt sections they are allowed to map against. Affected files: `src/cli/project.rs`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs`. Remediation: remove the milestone-scope filter from related-work selection and keep the same-milestone restriction only for generating short aliases.
   Rationale: The problem statement defines related work by label/subsystem overlap, not by milestone membership, and the routing layer is already set up to handle foreign canonical IDs safely.
2. `src/cli/project.rs:1893-1904` pre-marks direct relations beyond `NEARBY_DIRECT_DETAIL_PREFETCH_LIMIT` as unavailable, and `src/cli/project.rs:2899-2914` then drops any direct dependency/dependent whose detail was capped or whose `br show` failed. That means high-priority nearby owners can disappear from `## Nearby work` before the byte-budget logic ever runs, even though their IDs are already present on the focal bead itself. Because `planned-elsewhere` routing only whitelists IDs from `Already Planned Elsewhere` and `Nearby work`, those skipped direct relations become unroutable in review despite still being real adjacent work. Affected files: `src/cli/project.rs`. Remediation: keep rendering direct relations from bead/relation summary data when detail refresh fails or is capped, and use detail only to refine status/description when it is available.
   Rationale: Direct dependencies and direct dependents are the highest-priority nearby categories; silently removing them on transient lookup failures or arbitrary caps defeats the feature's stated prioritization and loses valid review-routing targets.
3. `src/cli/project.rs:418-421` now makes `project create-from-bead` fail on any `load_bead_summaries` error, and `src/cli/project.rs:1848-1853` maps a `br list` failure straight into that fatal path. Compared to the base branch's best-effort behavior, a transient `br list` problem now blocks bead-backed task creation entirely, even when `br show` can still resolve the active bead and its direct relations. That turns supplemental nearby-context hydration into a hard bootstrap dependency and makes task startup much more brittle. Affected files: `src/cli/project.rs`. Remediation: keep corrupt-record failures fatal, but degrade ordinary `br list` IO/exit failures to an empty/partial nearby context so project creation still succeeds.
   Rationale: This is a user-visible availability regression in the main bead bootstrap path, not just an internal prompt-shaping detail.
